supportedCapabilities.summary: Load a property value and store it in an Akita scenario variable.
stepPatterns: сохранено значение "(.*)" из property файла в переменную "(.*)"
notes: The property name is resolved through PropertyLoader before storing the value in the scenario variable store.

supportedCapabilities.summary: Set a scenario variable from a literal or property-backed value.
notes: The second argument is resolved through PropertyLoader.getPropertyOrValue before storage.

supportedCapabilities.summary: Sleep for the requested number of seconds.
notes: The implementation delegates directly to Thread.sleep with second-to-millisecond conversion.

supportedCapabilities.summary: Load a template, replace placeholders from a Cucumber table, and store the filled content in a variable.
stepPatterns: шаблон "(.*)" заполнен данными из таблицы и сохранён в переменную "(.*)"
notes: If any requested placeholder is absent in the template, the step logs the miss and throws a RuntimeException.

supportedCapabilities.summary: Assert that two scenario variables are equal.
Assert that two scenario variables are not equal.
stepPatterns: значения в переменных "(.*)" и "(.*)" не совпадают

supportedCapabilities.summary: Delete a file with the requested name from the provided directory.
notes: The step throws when no matching file is found.

supportedCapabilities.summary: Assert that a scenario variable containing a PDF file includes the requested text fragment.
notes: Despite the generic step wording, the implementation constructs com.codeborne.pdftest.PDF from the variable value and checks extracted PDF text.

supportedCapabilities.summary: Assert that a scenario variable matches a property value, case-insensitively.
stepPatterns: значения из переменной "(.*)" и из property файла "(.*)" совпадают

supportedCapabilities.summary: Pass a boolean assertion expression through the Akita scenario evaluator.
notes: The implementation wraps the supplied text in assert(...) and delegates evaluation to akitaScenario.getVars().evaluate(...).
This bundle does not promise the full expression language beyond boolean assertions accepted by that evaluator.
